trainRoutes.notes: Low-cost morning commuter option with a straightforward connection to Colombo city buses.
Best option for commuters connecting to buses at Pettah or Fort.
Useful for evening travel back toward Colombo after office hours.

busRoutes.name: Pettah / Fort to Maharagama via Town Hall
busRoutes.frequency: Every 8 to 12 minutes in peak periods
busRoutes.notes: Reliable connector for travellers heading from Fort into Colombo 7.

busRoutes.name: Pettah to Kollupitiya / Borella corridor
busRoutes.notes: Alternative if Route 138 is crowded; still useful for central Colombo access.

busRoutes.name: Kiribathgoda to Angulana via Colombo city
busRoutes.notes: Useful for onward travel if the commuter reaches Pettah from another mode.

stationGuidance.transfer: Walk about 6 minutes from the railway station to the Pettah / Fort bus stand for Route 138 or 177 toward Town Hall and Colombo 7.
Take a short tuk-tuk or local bus toward Borella or Town Hall if you arrive at Maradana and need Colombo 7.

travelTips: For the cheapest trip from Veyangoda to Colombo 7, use a train into Colombo and connect by bus instead of taking a full-distance taxi.
If a train is delayed, the next-lowest-cost fallback is a bus transfer from the nearest major station rather than starting the entire trip by three-wheeler.
Safety incidents, derailments, or blocked accessibility features should always be escalated immediately and not treated as standard route inquiries.
